steps.title: Scale the second equation to match the first
Identify the inconsistency and solve
steps.content:
Compare to the first equation:
  • First equation: $-12x + 14y = 36$
  • Scaled second equation: $-12x + 14y = -36$
The left sides are identical but the right sides differ (36 ≠ -36). This is impossible. The same expression cannot equal two different values. The equations represent parallel lines that never intersect. That matches choice D — D is correct.
